reviews.review_text:
Chur bro
stayed here for a camp in 2022 and it was more awesome than having beers without beer hehehehe

Fantastic location to Canberra, and all the attractions of the A.C.T. The park and staff were friendly and very efficient, clearly the staff understand customer service and what is expected by tourists. I camped in the on the artificial grass, quite good, although the surface underneath surface was too hard for my pegs.
The brand new camp kitchen was spotless and warm, considering I stayed early May - so cold. Highly recommended to any age of visitor to A.C.T.

We stayed at Capital Country Holiday Park with Camp Quality for a family group weekend away.
We really enjoyed the park and facilities. The cabins are a little dated but comfortable and clean. The split system ac kept us nice and warm over night.
The kids had a wonderful time playing putt putt, soccer and baskets ball. It was too chilly to use the pool unfortunately. The staff were very friendly and made us all feel welcome.
The park facilities are well maintained and tidy.
Had a great experience there

Great caravan park away from the bussel of Canberra but close enough to travel in each day. Plenty of trees and ample sized sites, they also have a number of fenced pet friendly sites. The amenities are clean and tidy and the camp kitchens are great, make sure you stay when visiting Canberra.
Fantastic peaceful surrounds. Close to Canberra
Excellent clean tidy park. Good facilities
Not far from Canberra

We stayed at the Capital Country Holiday Park while on a Motorbike National Rally in Canberra
We stayed in a cabin for 3 nights
We catered for ourselves in the camp kitchen. Great place to stay and catch up with friends from all over Australia
Thank you for having us

Our caravan site is poorly maintained and is too close to the sites either side.
Amenities need a refresh. My car park is directly below large gum tree resulting in my car getting covered in bird crap each night. Roads within the park are in poor condition.
Staff friendly, amenities old but clean, enough room to dress.

You know every time I come here I feel so welcome & cared for!!
Every time I go to my room the lights are on and the heater is going and I feel like I'm being welcomed home!! The reservation process whether over the phone or on the Internet is smooth as silk. Arrival after hours is very efficient & safe.  The best thing is the bitumen caravan drive through sites and the food at the Eaglehawk Hotel!!
Water is drinkable but yucky.
Lovely lake, car wash bay, putt putt golf, mini soccer, fire pit, camp kitchens, school cabins,
Drive throughs are excellent and well maintained, and all are close to amenities that are clean but old and for some reason rusting!?
Plenty of cabins, powered sites - and these guys cater for schools - massive school cabins and camp kitchens.
"Dog Freedom" sites with fences around to keep your dog & van secure. Pet friendly but only 2 cabins and all caravan sites.
Heaps of powered sites are reasonably priced.
The only thing is the distance this place is a long way out of town, but it's not too far (11-25 minutes) to places like Dickson (11 mins) Gungahlin (15 minutes) Ainsley shops (15-20 minutes) Belconnen shopping mall & North Canberra.
Last time I stayed in a school cabin with a queen bed with a single bedroom - absolutely everything you need; very comfortable, quiet, good hot water, absolutely spotless wouldn't wouldn't go anywhere else!! This time I stayed in a standard cabin they're a bit cheaper $110, but they are a bit old, refreshed and still clean with all you need.
We stayed in an ensuite site, was incredible! Huge and so clean and comfortable. Would highly recommend
Great place close to Canberra and all it has to offer.

About 18 minutes out of town- cracking pub around the corner sells great steaks( proper good).

Amenities are clean and has a pool( Christ knows why, Canberra is as cold as a mother-in-law’s stare)- kids loved the soccer pitch in the tennis court , mini golf and basketball ball- we really liked it .

This will be where we stay from now on
The place is huge and really close to Canberra. Lots of amenities

I absolutely loved my stay here. The staff were very accommodating and welcoming, I extended my stay twice and they fixed the booking with a smile. The park itself is lovely, good facilities, quiet, safe and friendly. The dog freedom sites are fabulous, all the sites were roomy and comfortable. Quick drive into Canberra. Highly recommend to anyone visiting Canberra or passing through.

I am really surprised that this doesn’t have higher ratings. Yes, it is a 20 minute drive from town, and yes the amenities are old and are in need of refurbishment, but it was lovely and spacious, quiet (especially for January school holidays), had amazing hot water pressure, free mini putt, a playground, pool, and multiple camp kitchens and amenities blocks. Would definitely stay here again!

I am really surprised that this doesn’t have higher ratings. Yes, it is a 20 minute drive from town, and yes the amenities are old and are in need of refurbishment, but it was lovely and spacious, quiet (especially for January school holidays), had amazing hot water pressure, free mini putt, a playground, pool, and multiple camp kitchens and amenities blocks. Would definitely stay here again!

Early January camping holiday - Positives - clean and tidy park and pleasant large treed camping sites, friendly service; noted that for those taking dogs, there are special fenced in sites which seems like a great idea to let your dog roam in your own fenced site.

Negatives - amenities block could use an update; no shade over pool area; on a hot day I was looking forward to a morning swim before it was too hot and before kids hop in the pool - still being cleaned at 8.30 am; although not the fault of the park - the location is a 20 minute drive to main Canberra attractions as a sightseer.

Overall, the park looks clean and tidy with many bins placed around.

A great place
Plenty of room for our van
Clean toilets
Great place to stay, we used it as a base to visit Canberra. Only thing is we couldn't get wifi in the room but we could outside.

Receptionist clearly doesn’t like her job. She does what’s required but not a smile to be had. Park looks unloved and rundown. Sites not maintained. Gravel where there shouldn’t be. No gravel where there should be. Desperate need for a leaf blower. Lots of dead grass and dead plants. Three washing machines spread across a site this big is just ridiculous. You have to lug your washing round the park hoping one of them is empty.

Really tidy little park. It has enough to keep the kids occupied for some adult p & q and it's close to Canberra city and all its activities. We used this as a base to stay in our campervan and explore Canberra and it was perfect for that. Things are a little dated but still tidy, and it's a friendly little park that's very reasonably priced. We would definitely stay here again.

ATTENTION Dog Owners!!
BEST caravan site EVER at Capital Country Holiday Park!!!
While I understand why dogs have to be on a lead at all times in a caravan park, I imagine it's confusing for our pup to always have to be restrained outside when she's used to running around at home.
When I looked up this caravan park in Canberra and saw they had "Dog Freedom Sites" I was intrigued and excited. At only $10/night extra we got an enormous fenced in site which fit our caravan plus a huge area for our dog to run around in. My 8 year old really enjoyed playing with her outside and our dog clearly enjoyed it too, as evidenced by her constant zoomies every day!
Well done Capital Country on such a progressive vision for travelling pet owners 🐕🐩🦮🐕‍🦺

Book a standard 2 bedroom cabin, it can stay for 6 persons, comes with kitchen and living room, nice place to stay in woods, you will need to bring own body wash and shampoo as it is not provided, wild life spotted when going out, overall it is a peaceful place to stay.
Great campsite, clean amenities and a lovely camp kitchen. Short drive to the Park & Ride to explore Canberra also.

Filthy public bathroom amenities and laundries with mould in the tile grouting.
A LOT of mould.
They tried to say it was just old mould stains which is not true. Needs a lot of bleach and regrouting probably.
The staff didn't care at all.
Couldn't get out of there fast enough.
Black mould like that is a health hazard.

Attended a group gathering (club AGM) and the park was ideal for the needs of our club. Plenty of accommodation and parking, use of the camp kitchen for our meet and greet, and a well catered meal on Saturday evening. Just remember to BYO.
A fair way out of Canberra, but good rooms. Only issue was that the water tasted a bit odd. Enjoyed the mini golf course too.

One AC for two reasons and one lounge room. On top of AC was locked on 24 degree C. In such arrangement AC was unable to heat up the bedroom to normal temperature resulting a long cold sleepless night. 😢
Verry tidy and affordable caravan park...
Easy to deal with and easy to get to. Highly recommend

Lovely park located close to Canberra. Drive thru sites, powered sites, cabins, dog friendly, dog enclosure sites and clean amenities.
Friendly staff.
Plenty of things to do for kids.
Swimming pool, courts and play areas.
Easy to park your van.
No road noise at night.
The staff are fantastic and understanding of our needs. Accomodation is comfortable and clean.
Ok if you like caravan parks , a bit pricey.

The park is quite nice with a good play area for kids big and small, but the cabin(3 bed Rosella) was disappointing to say the least. Not a lot of room with 3 double beds and a bunk over the top(why).   A small tv for the 5 people.  The issue we had was the lounge and mattresses need replacing.   We needed to have pillows to cushion your back on the wooden frame of the lounge. The beds were very uncomfortable.  The cleaning was not good either.  Under the beds(and you can see it from the lounge) was piles of dust paper and a sock.  The floor seemed like it had not been washed.  It wasn't cheap either.  We were ending 4 months of travel mainly staying in park cabins and we would rate this as the second worst.
Spacious parking, a clean and tidy environment, and a beautiful view. We were at the power site, which made the stay even more convenient — truly a delightful place 👍

We had a lovely stay overnight. It was well appointed and we were allowed to have our 2 dogs inside the bungalow. Very well maintained,clean and tidy .No rough potholes,straight surfaces even for RVs. A GREAT PLACE and good pool and play equipment for the little humans too

This is a very nice park and in a beautiful location with wildlife about. Great facilities and fun things for the kids to do. Friendly service. We had a fenced site for our dog which was amazing.The only thing that let's it down is dated bathrooms with weak showers and police visited the permanent residents area every day of our stay although we never felt unsafe. Otherwise I would highly recommend and would stay here again if visiting the area.

Highly recommend
Fun place for the grandchildren
Rabbits..ducks..kangaroos
Birdlike
Easy laid back accommodation

We stayed in a lakeside villa which while a little dated had a great view over the lake and was a great place to stay after a trip from Batemans Bay and a day exploring Canberra on our way through to Sydney.
What a great place..lots  to do.staff just the best
Very good cvan park. Staff were very friendly and very helpful.

Our car club has stopped here for the last 3 years for Xmas in July, rooms are always spotlessly clean. Friday night is a BBQ and we are very fortunate to be able to use the conference room to eat our meal, Saturday night is in the dining room and we are served a delicious meal by Bill and his wonderful staff, can't thank them enough 😋 looking forward to next year.

The individual after the service was exceptionally unprofessional. Our Tesla ran out of battery and requested payment for the caravan spot that was hired for charging purposes. However, they refused to assist us when we were willing to pay. They were rude and unhelpful. We contacted our tow truck, but they still insisted that we leave without our car. It is unacceptable to be asked to leave without a means of transportation. This is a significantly worse experience than renting a spot and receiving poor service.
Has bore water for drinking,  its on the outskirts of Canberra but there is a petrol station and a pub close by.  Poor mobile coverage and WiFi doesnt have any coverage.

Stayed in onsuit site for caravan
Fantastic caravan park
Helen

We stayed here back in January, of 2025 it was a beautiful clean park and well worth staying at. Why I give it 5 stars is because while there I lost my wedding ring in the pool, I put in a lost and found notice and 3 months later I got  phone call saying they found it. They sent it back May of 2025 with no issue. This kind of customer service is rare and deserves top tier recognition ❤️ Thank you 💗🙏💗
A nice quiet park.

5 stars all the way for this jem.
I think this may be my go-to place whenever I'm visiting Canberra.
I had one of the smaller pet friendly cabins, which was cosy and clean. The staff here are really friendly and helpful, which made for an awesome stay.
Definitely recommend staying here.
There is no grass lots of dirt that you step out in very dusty then when it rains becomes muddy. Amenities need renovating. I recommend the Showground for caravans

The place has gone downhill they have Cameras everywhere hidden in all the lights I even seen them in trees.If you don't care about being watched the whole time your there then go to the sh-t whole. The roads have holes everywhere they probably have Cameras in them also. Hidden cameras in trees on the light poles under the cabins at the pool you are under surveillance 24/7 even if it feels like your not walk at night take pictures with your phone and you will see hundreds of cameras.
Beautiful park nice affordable accommodation.  A must visit.
Brilliant time upgraded to Lake cabin

Great place out of the rush of town. Great swimming pool. Super quiet. Follow us on YouTube
Sweet az RVN
A really lovely park with great facilities. Clean & tidy throughout with very pretty surroundings. Great place to stay for families. Also just a short drive to Canberra .

Lovely quiet caravan park located 20 minutes north of Canberra in Sutton.|Shower is huge (approximately 2.4 x 1.2M) ideal for a wheel chair etc.|Friendly staff .|Big concrete slab to park on.|Mini golf, pool, tennis etc.|Some cabins overlooking a lake.
A great park. Convenient and well kept. We will return.

A single top loader washing machine & a single dryer in the laundry at the far end & nearby reception a laundry with 2 of each. We stayed 2 weeks & saw many campers coming & going. Description of “grass site” is actually all gravel - probably better for preventing mud & erosion. Plenty of trees but with that comes plenty of bird poop - we tried to park our car under our awning to save it. Gravel for van & shade cloth on the ground for stepping out of the van or parking the car. Water claims to be spring but has a lot of minerals, leaving white residue & after a taste determined it still seems treated with chlorine, so we didn’t drink without filter. Staff were lovely. Area was fairly quiet but so many cars close together meant a bit of engine noise throughout the park. However, people were respectful & no rowdy crowds. Dedicated camp spots for those with furry friends to roam freely inside a fenced area. Rubbish bins near each site & collected often. Very clean amenities though a little run down, however not impacting comfort. A playground in sand, volleyball in sand, tennis/basketball court, swimming pool, putt-putt & several shaded tables & chairs. Plenty of rabbits around at night. Wifi was reliable. Several camp kitchens, enclosed & open to the air, 1 with a vending machine with snacks. A large good quality park overall with everything one needs. 20 minutes into Canberra city.
Fabulous weekend away for the CRAKK run

my family and I stayed at Capital country holiday park apon arrival I checked into reception the receptionist was friendly and helpful as I we to use the bathroom in office area I overheard the receptionist making racial comments about my Religion and clothing  I fell descrimante against it reeked my whole weekend. cabin was out dated and not very clean won't be returning to this holiday park.

Es gibt Mitarbeiter die ihre eigenen Preise haben. Die Kabinen bieten nicht das was sie anpreisen.  Der Flughafen ist direkt am Campingplatz und auf der anderen Seite die Autobahn.  Ruhe bekommt man hier nicht.  Den ganzen Tag knattert der kleine Pickup mit lauter Auspuffanlage und nervt die Leute.  Der Preis von 150 Dollar gerechtfertigt nicht den Komfort.  Sehr enttäuschent und ich komme nie wieder dorthin.
Stayed on a powered van site. Good value for money. Recommend change cleaning times of toilets.
非常漂亮！就是房间里没有洗发液护发素，是忘记了呢还是疏忽不好说，但是还是很想再来的！下次booking水边别墅

Wir waren 5 Nächte auf dem Campingplatz. Ohne Probleme konnten wir eine Nacht verlängern.  Canberra hat so viel zu bieten, wir brauchten einen Tag länger als geplant zum anschauen. Der Campingplatz ist ruhig und ca 16 km von der Stadt entfernt, dass ist kein Problem schnell ist man hin und her gefahren. Die Sanitäranlagen waren im großen und ganzen in Ordnung auch die Sauberkeit hat gepasst. |Nach unserer Meinung sollten die defekten Matten bei den Caravanplätzen (zerrissen) ausgetauscht oder entfernt werden. Die Matten sind Stolperfallen. Da kann man hängen bleiben und böse stürzen.

It was good overall, BUT! we found a note written under the mattress and it said something like I’m coming for you Rachel. We also had snotty tissues stuffed into our mattress. Food that we got served wasn’t good either. When we walked into our room there was sand everywhere on the floor. Surprising, we also found an old man standing outside of our room at 2:00 am. It was okay, but there walks are VERY thin. Tape water is also not drinkable, please bring your own or if your desperate walk 3km to the nearest fill up tap where we seen a snake and an abandoned kitchen. Thankyou for reading

I would say value for money. We booked cabin with 2 kids. The cabin was comfortable with basic amenities provided. The facility have a pool through water was not great, but good for hot summer. The common kitchen had no utensils just an oven and fridge. Few beds in our cabin had no bed sheets on them. The water in tap is not drinkable so please get your own. The location was quiet and you can enjoy a nice lake view. I would be happy to go again with bit more preparation . Bathrooms had only hand soap .
Lovely spot suited us

Very popular holiday park. I have always liked staying here. Very clean, neat and tidy. Now dog friendly.  Has had major structures and improvements done.   Park is looking great. Staff friendly. Lot more new caravan sites have been added with some sites having closed in fences for dogs. Very popular.

We stayed at an unpowered tent site, was good, loved the atmosphere. But we do not recommend the pool, our little one (1year) got a middle ear infection presumably due to the pool water.
We would stay there again but not using the pool again.
環境優美，非常寧靜的一個度假勝地，但設施比較適合年青人或小朋友，手機信號在房間內完全收唔到，要在屋外才有微弱訊號，這個住客要注意⚠️
Not Worth it for the price

Great place for couples and families with dogs.|A variety of accommodation types from unpowered to lake side cabins.|Very pet friendly and we had a dog freedom site.|Many activities to do from putt putt golf, beach volleyball, soccer and basketball.|Camp kitchens are well stocked and laundry facilities.|15 minutes from Canberra and all the attractions of Canberra.|The facilities are well maintained and all working.

We stayed on a van site. The park is lovely with everything you could want. The things provided for kids are fabulous- pool, putt putt, basketball, soccer goals, play equipment… the park is clean and the staff a lovely. The facilities are clean but very dated and in need of a serious makeover and that included the laundry. We were happy with our stay.
Loved sitting on balcony in morning feeding ducks. So relaxing. Could do with a bit of a clean though. But fabulous spot. On lake.

Very old out dated rooms.

I must say I was shocked and disappointed. I left a day early as I did not wish to stay a second night
We stayed one night in a rooftop. Lovely place to stay. Would be great for young families. Very tidy & pleasant. Thank you
Very nice caravan park.  Great amenities,  dog friendly sites.
Very nice park,lots of wildlife in the paddock opposite,lots of things to see.Must visit 👍

Did not use a cabin, used a caravan site, pricing was very competitive for the area, park amenities are slightly dated but very clean  and well maintained. A very good range of facilities for family use and the staff are very helpful. Park is also dog friendly and has dog friendly fenced sites. Recommend this park
Well laid out Park with old but clean amenities.  Friendly staff & easy drive through to Canberra.

We spent 4 nights here as part of our Year 6 Canberra experience. The rooms are a little dated but have everything one needs. The showers were hot, the  bed was comfortable and the room was warm. On the colder nights an extra blanket was required though. Eating in the dining room was where the Holiday Park excels. Great food and plenty of it.Tasty and what you wanted after a day exploring Canberra with 140 students.

Rooms are fine, Service is okay, The heater doesn’t heat properly as its so cold in the area. No toothpaste, slippers. But beds are good, Separate rooms but very small. Each room has 2 beds, 1 on the top and a queen sized. Overall it was 3.5/5 in my opinion.

The cabin was ok but the bedrooms were freezing . No carpet on the floors and no electric blankets . So cold . Also ensuites were so tiny you couldntstand at the basin with the door shut . . We left a day early as my friend fell in the minute shower and hurt her knee . So hospital visit and then home . We were really looking forward to our holiday in Canberra but this experience really was unfortunate  . We always stay in cabins were ever we go and sadly this one was a terrible experience . Always give orther caravan parks a 10 but this one only one star .
The shower ,toilets and laundry are woeful only one washing machine and dryer showers are old
Large sites has dog friendly enclosed sites lots for families to do facilities are A bit outdated staff friendly
Amazing for kids

We always stay at the dog free sites when we can. This place is brilliant , so many activities for children and a  quiet leafy location for relaxing. Part from the cold. (We are from QLD) and not used to the weather it feels like home when we get here. Dogs love it. The best place we have ever stayed at for dogs. Other campgrounds should follow there example. Yes there are a few cobwebs in the showers but jut adds to the atmosphere. People that have negative comments, obviously haven’t stayed in some of the dreadful places we have encountered.

We were stuck in Port Macquarie due to heavy rains and flooding in NSW. They refused to refund our booking even when we advised we cannot travel as the NSE and NSW govt had advised ppl not to venture out. Very disappointed. Very rudely spoken and no compassion when we advised we cannot travel with 2 kids due to danger to our lives. Didn't make a difference.

On arrival the reception lady was very helpful,and trys help out,and she was GREAT. UNFORTUNATELY, we  stayed in a lake view cabin,it must have serious foundation issues,as the cabin is NOT level, the internal doors don't stay open the shower leaks,the microwave turntable derailed, a cup ended up against the side, after 30 seconds to warm up cup of milk,for breakfast, not to mention you end up rolling in to your partner in bed. This park is in a lovely location,and there was a big wedding held in there function room. No problem at all
With noise etc...we hoped they had a WONDERFUL DAY.
Maybe the abundance of rabbits everywhere MAY have effected the foundation due to there digging under the cabin.

Dit park is voor hondenliefhebbers een prima plek om te staan met de caravan/camper ook kun je hier huisjes huren die prima ingericht zijn.|Er is een zwembad en leuk mini midgetgolf baan , speelveldje,de camping keuken is prima en goed schoon.|Het personeel is ook vriendelijk.
Had our own caravan.. great sites heaps of room.
Great location, close to Canberra (15mins). We stayed in our caravan on a powered site. Good clean facilities

Had a few days at Capital COuntry in their new caravan ensuite. Huge ensuite and very clean. The caravan Park is older. The entertainment area (pool, courts, mini golf, camp kitchen) is newer and well kept. Great location for in and out to Canberra. It was quiet at the time we were there. Cool Steak House (Eagle Hawk Hotel) just down the road for meals Franklin is a quick drive for Woolworths and Chemist and a few takeaway places. Overall we enjoyed our stay.
Nice and quiet with drive through sites and an easy drive into Canberra.
A little fat out of your keen on seeing act capital. But quiet and roomy. Not pushed in like sardines
Nice park good size for vans. Enjoyed my stay

Very nice park. Good facilities reasonable prices for 2024 where greed has completely taken over any rationale in many caravan parks who charge $70-$90 per night, we simply avoid any caravan park that charges more than $45 per night for a powered site.
Powered Van site for 4 nights , pleasant and clean , a little bit of road noise on Saturday night  , Pet friendly , Ammenities are clean but do need a bit of a touch up , Really couldn’t go wrong 👍
Great stay, fantastic amenities.
Nice facilities. Roomy campsites

I only spent one night here while visiting the Egyptian Exhibition, but it fulfilled all my requirements. The cabin was large with 2 bathrooms.  The only problem was the remote controls for TV and Aircon. I had a lot of trouble using both. They possibly require new batteries or new remotes. The beds were comfortable and apart from no ice cube trays their was plenty of kitchen utensils etc.

The park itself was tidy but the buildings were desperately in need of repair. It was strange having a shower on missing tiles and looking up and seeing cobwebs and dust as there were no ceilings. The front office staff were great and the location was nice.

There is so much to do both at the holiday park and out and about in Canberra for all ages including mini golf, a swimming pool, volleyball, soccer, cricket and a playground . The sites were quite good with the majority of them having trees.
A Great place to stay when Attending Summernats, clean Amenities. Carn't comment on rooms as i bring my caravan.

Nice cabin, however the bathroom is a bit squishy and the wifi kept dropping in and out.  phone reception was not good. Nice grounds with plenty of things for the kids to do including mini golf and a pool

Defintely don't consider this park for a family appropriate holiday in January, as they host participants for the Summernats Festival in Canberra. We had to put up with revving engines and cruising musclecars, and the most abusive foul-mouthed people I have had the misfortune to encounter in all our travels. As long as they continue to support Summernats, we will not book there again!

This caravan park is less than 10mins from Exhibition Park. Check-in was fast and easy. It was super busy as it was the Summernats weekend. However, the park was pretty quiet come 10pm.
The female amenities were adequate and reasonably clean but the men's were less than adequate (which is understanding given the visitors).
I would recommend this caravan park to my friends.

Family stayed here for a short stay during holidays. It was an easy location in and out of city centre and kids enjoyed being on a villa by the lake. Villa was quite dated inside with furnishings but still enough room and comfortable for a family of 5.
Matress was too hard there is no handrail to get up the steps to the cabin
Nice place to take a campervan. Pool, putt-putt, tennis for kids. Nice surroundings.
Nice Clean villa was fabulous for my.family of 5. Had a bathroom in each bedroom was super! Very handy. Easy fuss free booking and service. Will definitely stay again

The rooms were not clean, there is mold all on the bathroom floor and walls. Moldy food left in bedrooms, we found medication left under a bedroom mattress. Old chewies, snot under the bed and the toilet floorboard has a urine odour. The bedding is not clean and left dirty and there is writing on the walls. The TV does not work. (School cabins)

Great price and value! Stayed here for 2 nights as we were going to a music festival, we were only 10mins from exhibition park which made travelling so easy, taxis were coming and going so we just had to jump in. The park had a nice pond upon entry, our cabin was clean and perfect for our couple night stay.
Multiple van parking lots of kid’s activities amenities need updating although clean
Great place to stay and staff are great.

Stayed 5 nights in an enclosed yard site for dogs. What a fantastic idea. Dogs loved the open space and running around. We loved not having to worry about them. Great park. All the amenities you need for a nice rest . A little tired in places but management doing their best to upgrade. A little out of town but still had  great time touring the sights. Lots to see and do. Any age and budget can stay here comfortably.

Not as advertised stayed here earlier in the year and was disappointed to find there was no entertainment for the kids. Tennis court was closed up, jumping pillow gone, mini golf in poor conditions. Paid what seemed full price, and a bit of a slap in the face to see advertising for what they had planned for the park (that looks great, but unfortunately for our stay we got nothing). Amenities were ok but very out dated and in some cases in poor conditions, seemed clean though. Positives were the dog sites which we used for our dog and was very happy with although be mindful that our site had a large gap under fence we had to block with a box.

This park makes you feel like you're on a lovely estate with duck ponds and pathways, and bunny's hopping around along the fenceline! Amenities are old with a slight odour but thats expected from boar water and septic, but they are very clean. Absolutely loved staying here and definitely a place I'd return to!

Kids were here for a school camp. Amenities look great, plenty of open space and things for people to do. However, on a Monday, the reception was closed at 5pm!!! The man who finally answered was grumpy and abrupt.

5 star 🌟
Amazing place
Very clean
Friendly staff

Needs a serious update, amenities very average. Park full of school groups, very noisy early mornings and evenings. Lots of school buses parking in front of drive through sites leaving their engines running for extended periods. Grounds need maintenance, neatest part is entrance. Promises a lot but sadly doesn't deliver. Wouldn't stay here again.

Would not stay at this place again.
No wifi. TV reception hit and miss. Sometimes you couldn’t get any programmes.
Bin had no lid. Mould on the ceiling in the bathroom. Tap in basin very dirty.
Only one three seater settee which looked grubby.
Whole place very tired. Could do with a complete upgrade.
Had a pet friendly powered site, park was neat and tidy, unfortunately amenities were lacking updates,
Lovely staff.  Clean and good facilities for children

Arrived early, and we were allowed to check in, which was appreciated. Cabin we stayed in was very old and tired and shows every bit of its age.  When wiping the benchtop before dinner,  brown coloured dirt, which I believe was the laminate finish  on every bench coming off. Table top when wiped went a white milky colour, which appeared to be leftover cleaning product which took 3 cleans to get off.  When we opened the cupboards to get crockery out, most of it  was covered with dead ants as well as dead ants on the shelves.  By this time, it was after the 3:00pm office close time, so our only option was to wash all the crockery, cutlery, and cooking pot we planned on using in our 3 night stay.

Whilst our stay was in Spring, overnight temperatures went down to 2 degrees.  The reverse cycle air conditioner did warm the main area, but it did little if anything for the remainder of the cabin.

Park runs on bore water, which is safe to drink, and they do offer customers tank water, available for collection at the main reception building. Otherwise, stock up on bottled water before you arrive.

Location to Canberra isn't too bad, only 15km away,

Very old & dirty/mouldy amenities. Function in main room, possibly wedding, guests were in courts/facilities all night very disruptive, loud & disturbing. Loud music,clapping,cheering.Adults teenagers,young children roaming everywhere talking yelling. Guests cars parked everywhere, under clothes line, lining the whole road thru park. Tried to contact management via text on emergency number...no reply.. 11pm still no sleep.....

Great price and place to stay!! The cabins were well set out with two bedrooms and two bathrooms!!! Lounge room in the middle that was well presented and equipped!! Really recommend and carport for bike/car protection.
Nice shady Park, helpful staff, stayed a week and  would stay again.

have stayed here before  and were going to stay here in a few weeks time but needed to cancel our site booking 2 weeks before going due to personal reasons and they had no problem with returning our money. Having stayed here a couple of times and having also lived in Canberra we will always come back to this park as its so quiet Might not be in the middle of town but its a straight drive in and also a good spot if wanting to visit Sydney for the day. When we lived there we used to leave Saturdays at 6am be in the middle of town by 9 and leave at 5  home by 8 and have a great day out Its a straight drive into town on a duel highway

Fantastic park very clean dog friendly
Stayed in a cabin was set out well
Just great.

5 stars all round.
Well kept park.
Well priced.
Great fenced dogs yards.
Everything was clean and tidy and staff were lovely

It was good for the most part, but the food was revolting. My school took us here for camp and I must say the food was unappealing and unappetising, I had to wait half an hour in the bathroom waiting to vomit. Food was cold, dry, under or overcooked. Inedible. Cabins had holes in the walls, blankets were way too thin as I couldn’t get any sleep and the time schedule was way too early for breakfast. Would not recommend.

Certainly adequate for our needs. Warm enough when -5C overnight. Lovely seeing birds and bunnies around.
Certainly will return.

Not good ... but cheap!
Apart from the dirty rooms they use bore water, with a possibility to fill up.water bottles at a rainwater tank.

Rooms were clean and tidy. But there was no internet at all. It was advertised that internet will be available but it didn’t connect at all. No drinking water in villa as they are using boring water (that tastes weird)
Great place to stay, everytime in Canberra we head here, quiet, clean, and great ppl.

The entire park is run down and decrepit.The roads are horrible.The cabin we stayed in was disgusting,dirty and very old oven etc.The entire park needs to be demolished and rebuilt,as it's not suitable for accomodation .DO NOT WASTE YOUR MONEY staying in this dump

The price is reasonable, but the cabin (where we were)  needs proper cleaning. It's very dirty. Dust anywhere - behind the door (been sitting there for ages) , bedside table, floor, toilet - needs disinfectant.

Hygiene is important.

Great little caravan park out of town but easy access to all areas of the ACT and main roads. Quiet area. They're currently updating some facilities and they're going to be great. Very friendly and helpful staff. Had a very cold night there and we were supplied with extra heating.

Bill was very rude, the sheets were dirty he was more interested in talking to some blonde lady that works there he completely ignored my request for new sheets I went for the car show. rooms were advertised at $130 a night I paid $1000 for four nights. I think capital country needs a new manger seems like he's pocketing the money the tennis courts were dirt with a net. They had no jumping pillow as advertised for my children the place was full of drunks it's no place for children. Not to mention the roads had to replace 2 flat tires in 4 days because of the big pot holes in the road and a wheel alignment.
Caravan park very run down, ablutions really old and worst we have seen on trip around Australia.
